purpose: Define the repeatable criteria for promoting listed companies into the 25-company first paper/product reference slice.

promotionCriteria.name: listed-and-identifier-ready
promotionCriteria.rule: Prefer listed companies with KRX code, OpenDART corp code, aliases, and stable companyId.

promotionCriteria.name: group-representative
promotionCriteria.rule: The company should represent a material part of the group's public investment narrative.

promotionCriteria.name: sector-diversifying
promotionCriteria.rule: The company should add a distinct business axis rather than duplicate an already covered exposure.

promotionCriteria.name: cross-group-comparable
promotionCriteria.rule: The company should help compare similar roles across groups, such as semiconductors, batteries, mobility, telecom, components, logistics, defense, or holding-company capital allocation.

promotionCriteria.name: public-source-available
promotionCriteria.rule: Annual report, DART filing URL, recent earnings material, investor presentation, value-up plan, or shareholder-return material should be obtainable.

promotionCriteria.name: runtime-claim-feasible
promotionCriteria.rule: Claims should be promotable with companyId, companyScope, period/date, source URL or filing ID, evidence locator, and verification state.

promotionCriteria.name: investor-facing-relevance
promotionCriteria.rule: The company should naturally support investor questions about earnings, capital allocation, risk, business pipeline, valuation context, or market signal.

promotionCriteria.name: scope-control
promotionCriteria.rule: Avoid selecting companies that would require a new accounting framework, a new regulated-product domain, or broad subjective investment advice in the first slice.

samsung.rationale: Core semiconductor and electronics anchor; primary market signal for Samsung exposure.
Battery and energy-storage axis; comparable to LG Energy Solution and SK Innovation battery exposure.
Holding, construction, trading, and capital-allocation axis; useful for portfolio-level interpretation.
Bio/CDMO growth axis; adds a non-electronics high-growth vertical.
Component layer such as MLCC and camera modules; improves comparability with LG Innotek and Hyundai Mobis.

sk.rationale: Semiconductor and HBM anchor; core market narrative for SK.
Energy, battery, and transition exposure; captures restructuring and energy-cycle issues.
Telecom, AI, data-center, and stable cash-flow axis.
ICT investment and platform portfolio axis; connects portfolio structure with SK Hynix exposure.

hyundai-motor.rationale: Primary OEM anchor and main market signal.
Second OEM, product-mix, profitability, and global sales comparison axis.
Core parts, electrification, software, and module supply-chain axis.
Logistics, finished-vehicle shipping, and value-chain resilience axis.
Defense, rail, and export-growth axis; broadens beyond passenger vehicles.

lg.rationale: Components, camera modules, and vehicle electronics; comparable to Samsung Electro-Mechanics.
Telecom, data, and stable cash-flow axis.

hanwha.rationale: Reference issuer connecting DART/KRX records with group-level briefing.
Defense and aerospace anchor; central to recent Hanwha investment narrative.
Defense IT, radar, satellite, and systems-integration axis.

paperWording: We use a fixed 25-company reference slice consisting of five listed companies from each of five Korean business groups. The slice is selected to maximize group representativeness, sector diversity, public-source availability, and cross-group comparability while keeping the source-to-claim harness auditable. The slice is not intended to be exhaustive; additional affiliates are retained as second-wave product and SCI-study expansion material.
